summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire and rare Thin Man make this show shine
blurb: The set features a standout Ballad Of A Thin Man and a scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain suite, plus a powerful second‑set flow with Space into So What and an electrifying Sugar Magnolia. Jerry delivers crisp, energetic guitar tones while Brent shines on keyboards during the jams. Overall a high‑energy late‑80s performance worth hearing.
review:
On March 27, 1988 at the Hampton Coliseum the Dead delivered one of their most exhilarating spring shows. The first set rolls through a tight sequence of bluesy rock – Iko Iko, Little Red Rooster and Stagger Lee – before bursting into a rare Ballad Of A Thin Man that fans have long coveted. From there the band rides a seamless groove through Cumberland Blues, Me And My Uncle and To Lay Me Down, each song charged with fresh energy.

The second set is where the night truly ignites. An opening Space segues straight into a bright Sugar Magnolia, then launches into the legendary Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain suite that has been described as "wildly energetic" and "blowing a gasket." The momentum carries through an inspired Estimated Prophet, a soaring Eyes Of The World, and a deep‑driven drum duet before another Space leads into the exuberant Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad → I Need A Miracle. Highlights like the scorching Scarlet>Fire, the tight jam on Space/So What, and Brent’s fireworks on keyboards make this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead, especially when paired with the crystal‑clear FM‑board recording that captures every nuance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Eight of ten recordings label the show as positive, repeatedly praising the Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain suite, the rare Ballad Of A Thin Man, and high‑energy jams like Space/So What. Reviewers use terms such as 'scorching', 'wildly energetic', 'blowing a gasket', and 'superb' across multiple sources, indicating strong consensus of an outstanding performance.

best_recording.reason: Excellent FM‑board source with clear, fresh EQ and no drop‑outs; captures the full two‑set performance crisply.

key_highlights: Rare Ballad Of A Thin Man opens a tight first set.
Scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain suite drives the second set.
Space into So What and Sugar Magnolia deliver high‑energy jam flow.
Brent’s keyboard fireworks and deep, driving drum work elevate the improvisations.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers crisp, energetic guitar tones throughout; highlights include a fiery solo on Me & My Uncle and an explosive lead during Scarlet Begonias > Fire.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation with a strong Estimated Prophet showcase; occasional mix issues wash out his low end but his playing remains reliable.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar is loud, crispy and drives the grooves; snarling vocal style shines on Sugar Magnolia, noted as record‑perfect by listeners.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work ignites during Scarlet Begonias > Fire ('Brent on fire') and adds character throughout; occasional wireless hiccup before Sugar Magnolia but overall stellar.
band_performance.Mickey: Deep, solid drumming provides a powerful backbone; heavy tom work especially drives the Scarlet>Fire and Space/So What passages.
band_performance.Billy: Drums are driving and dynamic, complementing Mickey's groove; the drum‑and‑audience mix is loud but adds excitement to the second set.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
